Former Thai PM Thaksin released after corruption sentence
description: Thailand’s former Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra has been released early from prison after serving eight months of a reduced one-year sentence linked to corruption and abuse of power cases. The 76-year-old political heavyweight will remain on probation until September and wear an electronic monitor during parole. Thaksin, who dominated Thai politics for years through his populist politics and the powerful Shinawatra political dynasty, still faces pending legal cases. His release has once again brought attention to Thailand’s deeply polarised political landscape.

description: Tamil Nadu's chief minister's swearing-in ceremony sparked controversy as the state song 'Tamil Thaai Vaazhthu' was placed third, after the national song and anthem. This deviation from tradition, where the state song usually begins government events, drew strong objections from CPI and the ruling TVK party, which vowed to revert to the established practice.

description: A Special Investigation Team is probing the murder of Suvendu Adhikari’s personal assistant, Chandranath Rath, with three arrests made and the investigation spanning multiple states. Investigators suspect hired sharpshooters, possibly from outside West Bengal, carried out the killing with military-style precision, suggesting a well-planned, professionally coordinated operation involving inter-state criminal links.

description: Congress leader Shashi Tharoor raised concerns about the 2026 West Bengal assembly elections, suggesting the Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls may have influenced the BJP's victory. He highlighted the removal of 91 lakh names, with 34 lakh appeals pending adjudication, questioning the fairness of the process given the BJP's 30 lakh vote margin.

description: Rajasthan Police's SOG is investigating alleged malpractices in NEET (UG) 2026 after the NTA received inputs about question sets overlapping with the actual exam paper. Investigators found over 100 biology and chemistry questions from a circulated set showing striking similarities to the NEET paper.

description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink refutes AI bubble concerns, citing a significant shortage in computing capacity, chips, and memory. He predicts a future where financial markets will trade futures of compute, highlighting the growing demand for AI infrastructure. BlackRock is actively investing in data centers and energy assets to meet this demand.

description: Urvil Patel’s explosive 65 off 23 balls against Lucknow Super Giants announced the arrival of CSK’s newest six-hitting sensation. The wicketkeeper-batter smashed six sixes within his first eight balls, rewrote IPL records. Backed by advice, and even a specially weighted bat from MS Dhoni, Urvil’s power-hitting philosophy is shaped by a gruelling routine of 200 practice sixes a day.

UP man crushes mother's lover to death with brick, attends wedding after killing to dodge suspicion
description: Lucknow Police have arrested a 22-year-old man and detained his minor relative for the brutal murder of a 28-year-old sanitation worker. The accused allegedly killed the victim over his relationship with the accused's mother, a liaison that had reportedly lasted for three years. The police cracked the case within 24 hours by analyzing CCTV footage and electronic surveillance.

title: English rhymes ‘Johnny Johnny’, ‘Rain Rain Go Away’ promote lying, selfishness: UP mantri
description: Uttar Pradesh Higher Education Minister Yogendra Upadhyay has sparked controversy by deeming popular English nursery rhymes 'Johnny Johnny, Yes Papa' and 'Rain Rain Go Away' as detrimental to Indian culture. He argued the rhymes promote lying and selfishness, contrasting them with Indian values of collective welfare and selfless joy.

title: After Bengal poll win, BJP’s fish fest to reassure voters
description: Following their swearing-in, BJP MLAs Agnimitra Paul, Dilip Ghosh, and Tapas Roy organized a 'fish festival' across Kolkata to counter perceptions of the party's aversion to non-vegetarian food. The events, attended by numerous party workers and locals, featured traditional 'machh-bhat' lunches, aiming to reassure voters about preserving Bengali culinary identity.

title: Transgender persons ineligible to avail surrogacy services: Centre
description: The Centre has clarified that transgender individuals, regardless of marital status, are ineligible for surrogacy services under the Surrogacy (Regulation) Act, 2021. This ruling came after Kerala inquired about a legally recognized transgender woman married to a man seeking surrogacy. The law currently restricts eligibility to specific categories of intending couples and women.

title: Rift in AIADMK widens as party seniors hold separate meetings; rebels set to back Vijay during floor test
description: AIADMK faces a deepening internal conflict as senior leaders C Ve Shanmugam and S P Velumani convened a meeting with MLAs, reportedly garnering significant support. This gathering occurred concurrently with a separate district secretaries meeting led by Edappadi K Palaniswami.

description: Former state women's commission chairperson Rupali Chakankar was questioned for six hours by a Special Investigation Team probing allegations against jailed astrologer Ashok Kharat. The SIT focused on her association with Kharat and his trust, with Chakankar stating she provided fitting answers. The inquiry is ongoing to determine the extent of her involvement.

description: Lucknow police arrested Vaibhav Bajpai for allegedly shooting BJYM member Chetan Tiwari, citing a long-standing personal rivalry and public humiliation as motives. Tiwari was shot outside his home with his wife and newborn present. The accused claims Tiwari spread defamatory rumors. Tiwari's condition is critical, with both legs unresponsive. Police are searching for associates.

description: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.
